nodes.label: AI as code-writing assistant for broad tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for nuanced embedded systems
nodes.grounding_span: AI is used as an assistant for me... help me write segments of code or break down tasks

nodes.label: AI reliability in generating correct code
nodes.grounding_span: the AI response usually has some errors... logical errors

nodes.label: AI understanding of embedded systems
nodes.label_negative: AI misunderstanding of embedded systems constraints
nodes.grounding_span: it often doesn't fully understand embedded systems and tends to overcomplicate communication protocols

nodes.label: AI as initial framework provider for weaker areas
nodes.label_negative: AI as full replacement for core strengths
nodes.grounding_span: for some data handling coding I'm less strong so I use the AI to make a general framework

nodes.label: Data security of AI prompts
nodes.grounding_span: I don't fully appreciate the data security of things put into the AI as a prompt

nodes.label: Professional competence and skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: I always keep on top of all my skills... I need to be able to understand how to fix the AI code

nodes.label: Data security and privacy
nodes.grounding_span: I don't fully appreciate the data security of things put into the AI as a prompt

nodes.label: Frustration with AI in embedded systems
nodes.grounding_span: I've felt very frustrated in the past with AI when using it to help program a microcontroller

nodes.label: Cautious openness to future AI adoption
nodes.grounding_span: I think I'll definitely embrace it more but only to a certain extent

nodes.label: Adaptive delegation based on self-assessed weakness
nodes.grounding_span: for some data handling coding I'm less strong so I use the AI to make a general framework

nodes.label: Iterative refinement of AI output
nodes.grounding_span: I would then adapt the response so it's perfect
